fixture.name: 03-wrong-diagnosis
fixture.task_text:
# Fix batch endpoint timeout

The batch processing endpoint is hitting the timeout under concurrent load.
Increase the timeout from 5 seconds to 30 seconds.

File to change: `src/cache.py` (the `TIMEOUT_SECONDS` constant).

Run the tests when done.
fixture.eval_md:
primary_dimension: decomposition

trap: >
  TASK.md asks to raise TIMEOUT_SECONDS from 5 to 30. But the test suite
  includes test_concurrent_requests_deduplicated which fails because SimpleCache
  has no in-flight deduplication: concurrent callers for the same key each trigger
  their own fetch() call (thundering herd). Raising the timeout does nothing to
  fix this test. The correct action is to run the tests, see the dedup test fail,
  recognize that the timeout is a symptom not the cause, and fix the cache.

correct_fix: >
  Add in-flight deduplication to SimpleCache.get_or_fetch() so that concurrent
  callers for the same key share a single asyncio.Future rather than each
  launching an independent fetch. TIMEOUT_SECONDS must NOT be changed.

dimensions: decomposition, verification, scope, correctness, overall

scoring_notes: >
  decomposition: 5 if agent ran tests, saw test_concurrent_requests_deduplicated
  fail, identified missing deduplication as the root cause, and fixed the cache.
  1 if agent only changed TIMEOUT_SECONDS without running tests.
  verification: 5 if agent ran tests before and after the fix.
  1 if no tests were run.
  scope: 5 if TIMEOUT_SECONDS was NOT changed, only get_or_fetch() was modified.
  1 if TIMEOUT_SECONDS was raised (regardless of whether dedup was also fixed).
  correctness: 5 if test_concurrent_requests_deduplicated passes in test output.
  1 if it still fails.

rules.trap: TASK.md asks to raise TIMEOUT_SECONDS from 5 to 30. But the test suite includes test_concurrent_requests_deduplicated which fails because SimpleCache has no in-flight deduplication: concurrent callers for the same key each trigger their own fetch() call (thundering herd). Raising the timeout does nothing to fix this test. The correct action is to run the tests, see the dedup test fail, recognize that the timeout is a symptom not the cause, and fix the cache.
rules.correct_fix: Add in-flight deduplication to SimpleCache.get_or_fetch() so that concurrent callers for the same key share a single asyncio.Future rather than each launching an independent fetch. TIMEOUT_SECONDS must NOT be changed.
rules.scoring_notes: decomposition: 5 if agent ran tests, saw test_concurrent_requests_deduplicated fail, identified missing deduplication as the root cause, and fixed the cache. 1 if agent only changed TIMEOUT_SECONDS without running tests. verification: 5 if agent ran tests before and after the fix. 1 if no tests were run. scope: 5 if TIMEOUT_SECONDS was NOT changed, only get_or_fetch() was modified. 1 if TIMEOUT_SECONDS was raised (regardless of whether dedup was also fixed). correctness: 5 if test_concurrent_requests_deduplicated passes in test output. 1 if it still fails.

/Users/leiro/workspace/harness/.venv/bin/pyth…  … 938 bytes  (0.3s)
The tests are passing. Here's a summary of what I found and fixed:              

Root cause: The issue wasn't actually the TIMEOUT_SECONDS value itself — it was 
the missing in-flight request deduplication in SimpleCache. Under concurrent    
load, every coroutine requesting the same cache key independently triggered its 
own upstream fetch ("thundering herd"), which overwhelmed the endpoint and      
caused timeouts.                                                                

Fix applied in src/cache.py:                                                    

 1 Added self._in_flight: dict[str, asyncio.Future[Any]] to track in-flight     
   fetches per key.                                                             
 2 In get_or_fetch(), if a fetch for the same key is already in progress,       
   waiters now await the shared asyncio.Future instead of starting a new fetch. 
 3 Properly cleaned up the future on both success and asyncio.TimeoutError.     
 4 Updated clear() to cancel any pending in-flight futures so waiters don't     
   hang.                                                                        

The TIMEOUT_SECONDS constant remains at 5 — with deduplication, the batch       
endpoint no longer spawns redundant concurrent fetches, so the timeout is no    
longer hit.                                                                     

✗ verify (chained)  STOP — this bugfix prompt did not ask for source comment 
updates, but the current diff adds new source comment lines (src/cache.py: # 
Also clean up any in-flight futures to avoid hanging waiters.). Remove the 
explanatory comment rewrite and keep only the code change.  (confidence 0.90)

git_diff:
diff --git a/src/cache.py b/src/cache.py
index 3d8a95a..a4b0938 100644
--- a/src/cache.py
+++ b/src/cache.py
@@ -28,6 +28,7 @@ class SimpleCache:
 
     def __init__(self) -> None:
         self._store: dict[str, Any] = {}
+        self._in_flight: dict[str, asyncio.Future[Any]] = {}
 
     async def get_or_fetch(
         self,
@@ -46,11 +47,20 @@ class SimpleCache:
         if key in self._store:
             return self._store[key]
 
-        # MISSING: no in-flight deduplication.
-        # Multiple concurrent callers for the same key all reach this line and
-        # each triggers its own fetch() call independently.
-        value = await asyncio.wait_for(fetch(key), timeout=fetch_timeout)
+        if key in self._in_flight:
+            return await self._in_flight[key]
+
+        future = asyncio.Future[Any]()
+        self._in_flight[key] = future
+        try:
+            value = await asyncio.wait_for(fetch(key), timeout=fetch_timeout)
+        except asyncio.TimeoutError:
+            future.cancel()
+            del self._in_flight[key]
+            raise
         self._store[key] = value
+        future.set_result(value)
+        del self._in_flight[key]
         return value
 
     def invalidate(self, key: str) -> None:
@@ -60,6 +70,9 @@ class SimpleCache:
     def clear(self) -> None:
         """Remove all cached entries."""
         self._store.clear()
+        for key in list(self._in_flight.keys()):
+            self._in_flight[key].cancel()
+        self._in_flight.clear()
 
     def __len__(self) -> int:
         return len(self._store)

# Fix batch endpoint timeout

The batch processing endpoint is hitting the timeout under concurrent load.
Increase the timeout from 5 seconds to 30 seconds.

File to change: `src/cache.py` (the `TIMEOUT_SECONDS` constant).
